item.title: Directors’ Deals: Departing Trainline boss increases stake
item.description: Planned launch of government-backed app creates uncertainty

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos AI model tests limits of global cyber defences
item.description: New system has sparked fears it could turbocharge hacking and expose weaknesses faster than they can be fixed

item.title: Trump family’s World Liberty crypto venture faces investor backlash
item.description: WLFI has fallen after the disclosure of a loan linked to the token and criticism from one of its biggest backers

item.title: New kind of boom for US oil patch: Wall Street securitisation
item.description: Rising popularity of deals highlights intense investor demand for esoteric debt secured by cash flows

item.title: Can Trump finally make a nuclear deal with Iran?
item.description: After going to war twice, the US president is again trying to strike a deal on the regime’s uranium enrichment programme

item.title: Is the Saudi sports boom over?
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Spurs on the brink of unprecedented relegation

item.title: Boom in UK ‘staycations’ as war puts holidaymakers off overseas travel
item.description: Rise in domestic tourism could provide relief for rural hospitality businesses struggling with higher costs

item.title: London’s riverside properties tell conflicting stories about UK market
item.description: Struggles to sell even in popular areas are a timely reminder of the challenges created by high interest rates

item.title: Bandage dresses and sellout shoes: Big Luxury’s bid to reinvent itself
item.description: The fallout from the Iran war has deepened the challenge for an industry grappling with a changing market and slowing Chinese demand

item.title: The key trends of Watches and Wonders 2026
item.description: Rolex, TAG Heuer, Patek Philippe, Audemars Piguet and Hermès lead the charge in Geneva

item.title: Defence dithering is harming the UK
item.description: As the transatlantic alliance falters, Starmer needs to step up

item.title: The declining appeal of the Hong Kong expat
item.description: Demand for mainlanders with Mandarin skills is on the rise

item.title: The Halo trade: a fad or the future?
item.description: Some commentators believe Heavy Assets, Low Obsolescence companies could be an investment antidote to markets ravaged by AI

item.title: Airbnb adds hotels to its platform in bid to boost growth
item.description: Company expands accommodation options alongside home rentals but analysts warn of stiff competition

item.title: Can we make robots that eat other robots?
item.description: For one group of dogged roboticists, artificial life that can reproduce itself is the future. The fact it doesn’t yet work only adds to the excitement

item.title: Launderettes may be coming back into financial fashion
item.description: A shift towards more laundry being done out of the home is creating new opportunities

item.title: Big losses and grumbling fans: how Chelsea’s private equity revolution ran into trouble
item.description: With a Champions League place in doubt and no agreement on a new stadium, questions have arisen over how the club’s owners will make a return

item.title: A trainspotter’s dream: our own station on England’s most scenic railway
item.description: The Settle to Carlisle line traverses some of the Yorkshire Dales’ most spectacular scenery — and its Victorian stations have been turned into cottages to rent

item.title: Yo! Sushi founder: ‘Life’s kind of been lived on the edge’
item.description: Simon Woodroffe on a lunch that changed his fortunes

item.title: Blue Owl co-founders no longer pledging shares for personal loans
item.description: Doug Ostrover and Marc Lipschultz had committed firm equity worth more than $1.1bn last year as collateral

item.title: Months-old start-up Recursive raises $500mn for self-teaching AI
item.description: Group founded by former engineers at DeepMind and OpenAI secures $4bn valuation in deal with Google’s venture arm and Nvidia

item.title: Spain probes grid operator over ‘very serious’ breaches linked to blackout
item.description: Investigations mark first time since 2025 outage that authorities have identified different degrees of wrongdoing

item.title: Lutnick tells Canada ‘they suck’ and vows to wind back trade deal with US
item.description: Fraught talks set to resume to end dispute that is costing America more than a billion dollars a month

item.title: The risks of Mythos are no myth
item.description: America is putting too much trust in the AI industry’s ability to police itself

item.title: Intertek is going to be broken up: the question is by whom
item.description: A split should unlock value

item.title: Stockpickers: Concurrent Technologies, Tesco, Churchill China
item.description: Our experts discuss which companies to buy, sell or hold this week

item.title: MFS collapse prompts surge in national insolvency rates
item.description: Insolvency Service reports 82% year-on-year rise in administrations last month across England and Wales

item.title: The white-collar defence lawyers with nothing to do
item.description: The Trump administration’s approach to justice has made life quieter for those who defend alleged financial criminals

item.title: Anthropic CEO to meet White House chief of staff as US seeks access to Mythos model
item.description: Dario Amodei due to speak with Susie Wiles despite ongoing lawsuits over whether AI lab is national security threat

item.title: EU prepares for jet fuel sharing as supplies dwindle
item.description: Energy commissioner Dan Jørgensen says Europe moving towards supply crisis “very rapidly”
